{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Tosa no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 = <!-- 家紋の画像 -->
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = {{ill|Tosa clan|ja|土佐氏}} (Ōshi-no-Atai)
|家祖 = {{ill|Kotachi-no-Sukune|ja|小立宿祢}}
|種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})
|出身地 = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|lt=Ōyamato Province|lt_ja=大倭国}} or {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}}
|根拠地 = Tosa Province (later {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}})
|人物 = See [[#People]]
|支流 = {{ill|Naga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|長国造}}, Naga-no-Abiko clan, {{ill|Oki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|意岐国造}}, Sumidō clan, Yosami-Abiko clan
}}
'''Tosa no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''tosa no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''tosa kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled [[#Territory|Tosa Province]] (the eastern part of the later {{ill|ritsuryō province|ja|令制国}} of {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}).

== Overview ==
=== Name ===
The "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' gives '''Tosa no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.

=== Ancestry ===
*According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, '''Kotachi-no-Sukunei''', 9th-generation descendant of '''{{ill|Mishima-no-Mizokui-no-Mikoto|ja|三嶋溝抗命|lt=Mishima-no-Mizokui-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=三嶋溝杭命}}''' — common ancestor with the Naga-no-Abiko (''naga no abiko'') — was appointed kuni no miyatsuko.

=== Clan ===
'''{{ill|Tosa clan|ja|土佐氏}}''' (''tosa-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (姓)|lt=Ōshi-no-Atai|lt_ja=凡直}}). After the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko system|ja|国造|lt=kuni no miyatsuko system|lt_ja=国造制}}, they served as {{ill|hyōtoku|ja|評督}} of Tosa-hyō ({{ill|Tosa District|ja|土佐郡|lt=Tosa District|lt_ja=都佐評}}), and later as {{ill|gunji|ja|郡司}} of Tosa District.

In the Nara period, they were said to have been called '''Ōshi-no-Atai'''. Since figures with the Ōshi-no-Atai surname appear as acting district administrators of {{ill|Aki District (Kōchi)|ja|安芸郡 (高知県)|lt=Aki District|lt_ja=安芸郡}}, it is clear that Aki District was under Tosa Province's rule<ref>{{ill|Ōta Akira|ja|太田亮}}, "Tosa," ''Seishi Kakei Daijiten'', Vol. 4, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店}}, 1963, p. 3954.</ref>. In addition, Kanyōda-no-Kimi, who was granted the {{ill|Kamo-no-Asomi|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|lt=Kamo surname|lt_ja=賀茂姓}} in the entry of {{ill|Jingo-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 2 ([[768]]), 11th month, ''bokushi'' day in the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', was also a local gōzoku, and the Tosa Taka-Kamo-Ōyashiro is said to have existed; it is identified with the {{ill|Engishiki-listed|ja|式内社|lt=Engishiki-listed|lt_ja=式内社}} {{ill|Tosa Shrine|ja|土佐神社|lt=Tosa-ni-masu Shrine|lt_ja=都佐坐神社}}.

== Base ==
The base of the kuni no miyatsuko was likely '''Tosa-gō''' in {{ill|Tosa District|ja|土佐郡}} of the later {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.

=== Territory ===
The western part of Tosa Province ({{ill|Hata District|ja|幡多郡}}) was ruled by the {{ill|Hata no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|波多国造}}, while the eastern part of Tosa Province, including {{ill|Aki District|ja|安芸郡 (高知県)|lt=Aki District|lt_ja=安芸郡}}, is said to have been ruled by the Tosa no Kuni no Miyatsuko. Regarding the name, there are theories that it derives from a contraction of "bin-sō" (agile and acute), reflecting the strong, brave, and martial spirit suggested by {{ill|Takeyori-wake|ja|国産み|lt=Takeyori-wake|lt_ja=建依別}}, and theories that it is based on the topographical features of the area, such as ''tōsa'' (distant narrow), ''tosa'' (earth narrow), or ''tori'' (place apart).

== Tutelary Shrine ==
Possibly '''{{ill|Tosa Shrine|ja|土佐神社|lt=Tosa-ni-masu Shrine|lt_ja=都佐坐神社}}''' (''tosa-ni-masu-jinja''), the {{ill|ichi-no-miya|ja|一宮|lt=ichi-no-miya|lt_ja=一宮}} of {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}} located in former {{ill|Tosa District|ja|土佐郡}}.

=== Related Shrines ===
*{{ill|Katsuragiokami Shrine|ja|葛木男神社}} (''katsuragi-o-jinja'')
*:An {{ill|Engishiki-listed shrine|ja|式内社}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|lt=Kōchi|lt_ja=高知市}}, where Katsuragi-me Shrine has been combined. Currently {{ill|Katsuragi-no-Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} and {{ill|Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}} are enshrined as deities, but it is thought that originally {{ill|Ajisuki-takahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, the ancestor of the kuni no miyatsuko, was enshrined<ref name="houga" />.
*{{ill|Asakura Shrine (Kōchi)|ja|朝倉神社 (高知市)|lt=Asakura Shrine|lt_ja=朝倉神社}} (''asakura-jinja'')
*:An {{ill|Engishiki-listed shrine|ja|式内社}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|lt=Kōchi|lt_ja=高知市}}, enshrining {{ill|Amatsuhahagami|ja|天津羽羽神|lt=Amatsuhahagami|lt_ja=天津羽羽神}}, the ancestor of the kuni no miyatsuko. Ni-no-miya of {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
*{{ill|Ueda Shrine|ja|殖田神社}} (''ueda-jinja'')
*:An {{ill|Engishiki-listed shrine|ja|式内社}} in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|lt=Nankoku|lt_ja=南国市}}, enshrining Ajisuki-takahikone-no-Mikoto.

== People ==
The full picture of those who held the position of kuni no miyatsuko is unknown.

== Descendants ==
*Tosa-no-Nosue
*:Grandson of Enuma, the kuni no miyatsuko, and son of Wani. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|lt=Empress Suiko|lt_ja=推古朝}}, he became {{ill|hyōtoku|ja|評督}} of Tosa-hyō ({{ill|Tosa District|ja|土佐郡|lt=Tosa District|lt_ja=都佐評}})<ref name="nakata2" />.
*Tosa Shigetaka (Tosa Shinpachirō)
*:15th-generation descendant of Tosa-no-Nosue, son of Motoyama Shigehiro, founder of the Tosa clan<ref name="nakata2" />.
*Motoyama Sanenari (Motoyama Tarō)
*:16th-generation descendant of Tosa-no-Nosue, son of Motoyama Shigekado. Followed the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}} and died in battle at the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}}<ref name="nakata2" />.

== Genealogy ==
=== Genealogy 1 ===
<div style="margin: 0px; padding: 2px; border: 1px solid #a2a9b1; text-align: center; border-collapse: collapse; font-size: 95%; text-align:center">
{{familytree/start}}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | | | | | | |001='''{{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大国主神}}'''}}
{{familytree|border=0| |)|-|-|-|.| | | | | | }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | 002 |001='''{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主神}}'''|002={{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}<br />(Ancestor of the {{ill|Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|洲羽国造}}, {{ill|Kiso no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|木蘇国造}}, etc.)}}
{{familytree|border=0| |)|-|-|-|v|-|-|-|-|v|-|-|-|-|.| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | 002 | | | 003 | | | 004 |001='''{{ill|Ame-no-Yaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天八現津彦命}}'''|002={{ill|Kushi-mikata-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}<br/>(Ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa-no-Kimi|ja|三輪氏|lt=Miwa-no-Kimi|lt_ja=三輪君}}, {{ill|Kamo-no-Kimi|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|lt=Kamo-no-Kimi|lt_ja=鴨君}}, {{ill|Munakata-no-Kimi|ja|宗像氏|lt=Munakata-no-Kimi|lt_ja=胸形君}}, etc.)|003={{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|ja|媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛}}<br />(Consort of {{ill|Emperor Jinmu|ja|神武天皇}})|004={{ill|Isuzuyori-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|五十鈴依姫命}}<br />(Consort of {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}})}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | | | }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | |001='''Mimatsuhiko-iroto-no-Mikoto'''}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | | | }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | |001='''Ōhibara-tomi-no-Mikoto'''}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | | | }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | |001='''Takemina-no-Mikoto'''}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | | | }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | |001='''Mikao-tachi-no-Mikoto'''}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | | | }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | |001='''Mashi-no-Mikoto'''}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | | | }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | |001='''Tameitsuko-no-Mikoto'''}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | | | }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | |001='''Imiki-no-Sukune'''<br />(Imoke-no-Sukune)}}
{{familytree|border=0| |)|-|-|-|v|-|-|-|-|.| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | 002 | | | 003 |001='''Kotachi-no-Sukune'''<br />(appointed '''Tosa no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|lt=Emperor Seimu|lt_ja=成務朝}})|002=Usakitari-no-Sukunei|003=Karase-no-Sukune<br />(appointed {{ill|Naga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|長国造}} during the same reign)}}
{{familytree/end}}
</div>

=== Genealogy 2 ===
<div style="margin: 0px; padding: 2px; border: 1px solid #a2a9b1; text-align: center; border-collapse: collapse; font-size: 95%; text-align:center">
{{familytree/start}}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | |001='''{{ill|Ame-no-Yaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天八現津彦命}}'''<br/>(Also known as Mimatsuhiko-iroto-no-Mikoto)}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | | | }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | |001='''Takehiwake-no-Mikoto'''}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | | | }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | |001='''Iroto-no-Mikoto'''}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | | | }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | |001='''Yagami-no-Mikoto'''}}
{{familytree|border=0| |)|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|.| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | | | | | 002 |001='''Furune-no-Mikoto'''|002=Oshikoro-no-Mikoto}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | | | | | 002 |001='''Momoso-hiko-no-Mikoto'''|002=Akame-hiko-no-Mikoto}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | | | | | 002 |001='''Kawashiri-hiko-no-Mikoto'''|002=Wakatsune-no-Mikoto}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | |,|-|-|-|-|(| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | 002 | | | 003 |001='''Morote-no-Sukunei'''|002=Ōmichi-no-Mikoto|003=Iwakowake-no-Mikoto}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | |!| | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | 002 | | | 003 |001='''Kotachi-no-Sukune'''<br />(appointed '''Tosa no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|lt=Emperor Seimu|lt_ja=成務朝}})|002=Imoke-no-Sukunei<br >(Ancestor of Naga-no-Abiko)|003=Eto-no-Mikoto}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | | | | | 002 |001='''Tomi-no-Sukune'''|002=Karase-no-Sukune<br />(appointed {{ill|Naga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|長国造}} during the reign of Emperor Seimu)}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | | | | | 002 |001='''Akaiko'''|002=Takao-no-Sukune}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | | | | | 002 |001='''Tsutsuko'''|002=Takane-hiko}}
{{familytree|border=0| |)|-|-|-|.| | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | 002 | | | 003 |001='''Enuma'''<br />(served as {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|lt=Emperor Buretsu|lt_ja=武烈朝}})|002=Kome|003=Kawanushi}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | |!| | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | 002 | | | 003 |001='''Mao'''|002=Tsutsumiko|003=Kishima-hiko}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | | | | | 002 |001='''Wani'''|002=Ekoma}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | | | | | 002 |001='''Nosue'''<br />(Tosa-hyō-toku, served during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|lt=Empress Suiko|lt_ja=推古朝}})|002=Akai}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | | | | | 002 |001='''Kukuri'''<br />(same hyō-toku, served during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|lt=Emperor Jomei|lt_ja=舒明朝}})|002=Kuragaki}}
{{familytree|border=0| |!| | | | | | | | |!| }}
{{familytree|border=0| 001 | | | | | | | 002 |001='''Matsuru'''<br />({{ill|Tosa District|ja|土佐郡|lt=Tosa District|lt_ja=都佐郡}} Daryō)|002=Takehiko}}
{{familytree/end}}
</div>

== Footnotes ==
{{Notelist}}
{{Reflist|refs=
<ref name="nakata2">Nakata Norinobu, "Tosa Motoyama-shi," ''Shokeifu'', Vol. 16.</ref>
<ref name="houga">Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/arisimata/arisima3.htm Ancient Shrines and Their Officiants in Central Tosa]," ''Koju Kinobōma'', 1998.</ref>
}}

== References ==
* Katō Kenkichi et al. (eds.), ''Nihon Kodai-shi Chimei Jiten'', Yūzankaku, 2007, ISBN 978-4-639-01995-4, pp. 786, 789

{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:とさのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:土佐国]]
[[Category:高知県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:都佐国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
